Title: Fix prompt drift — add promptLint() deduplication to auditor
Description:
The auditor's reflection system has caused severe prompt degradation across all agent prompts. Evidence: `prompts/00-architect.txt` has sentences repeated 6 times in a row ("Provide concrete evidence..." and "Ensure that all outputs are complete...").

**Requirements:**
* Add a `promptLint(promptText)` function that detects and removes duplicate sentences/paragraphs before the auditor commits any reflection update
* Before applying a reflection update in `agents/99-slack-gateway.js` `applyReflectionChanges()`, check for substring or cosine similarity match against existing prompt text
* Add a max prompt size budget per agent (2000 tokens) — reject reflection updates that would exceed it
* Log a warning when deduplication removes text: `log.warn('Removed N duplicate sentences from prompt')`

**Files:** `agents/11-auditor.js`, `agents/99-slack-gateway.js` (lines 885-946)
**Dependencies:** core/logger.js

Title: Clean all 12 prompt files — remove existing duplicates and drift
Description:
Manually audit and clean all 12 prompt files in `prompts/` to remove duplicated text caused by the auditor's reflection system.

**Requirements:**
* Review each file in `prompts/*.txt` for repeated sentences, paragraphs, or instructions
* Remove exact and near-exact duplicates while preserving the original intent
* Consolidate multiple "Reflection Update" sections into a single coherent section per prompt
* Verify each cleaned prompt is under 2000 tokens
* Commit cleaned prompts with clear diff showing what was removed

**Files:** All files in `prompts/*.txt` (12 files)
**Dependencies:** None — this is a manual cleanup task

Title: Deprecate openclaw-memory.js — unify on LanceDB memory system
Description:
Two parallel memory systems exist. `00-architect.js` imports `readRecentContext`, `readTodayScreenActivity`, `readTodayAudioTranscripts` from `openclaw-memory.js` — a filesystem-based markdown store that duplicates what LanceDB already holds.

**Requirements:**
* Replace the 3 function calls in `agents/00-architect.js` with equivalent `searchAllContext()` calls from `core/memory.js`
* Add `@deprecated` JSDoc annotations to all exports in `core/openclaw-memory.js`
* Verify `scripts/ingest-context.js` is writing to the LanceDB `context_feed` table (it does — just confirm)
* Update any other files that import from `core/openclaw-memory.js`
* Remove openclaw-memory.js imports from `00-architect.js` morning/evening brief builders

**Files:** `core/openclaw-memory.js`, `agents/00-architect.js`
**Dependencies:** core/memory.js must have equivalent search functions

Title: Add embedding cache with LRU + TTL to core/memory.js
Description:
Every `searchMemory()`, `searchScreenContext()`, and `addMemory()` call hits OpenRouter for embeddings. The same queries (e.g., each agent's `contextQuery`) get re-embedded on every `run()`. The morning brief alone generates 10+ embedding requests.

**Requirements:**
* Install `lru-cache` npm package
* Add an LRU cache in `core/memory.js` keyed on first 200 chars of the text being embedded
* TTL: 6 hours for query embeddings, 24 hours for content embeddings
* Max cache size: 1000 entries
* Persist cache to `~/hydra-brain/brain/embedding_cache.json` on graceful shutdown (SIGTERM)
* Reload cache from file on startup if it exists and is < 24 hours old
* Log cache hit rate every hour: `log.info('Embedding cache hit rate: X%')`

**Files:** `core/memory.js` (function `getEmbedding`)
**Dependencies:** lru-cache (npm)

Title: Encrypt SahibaBot namespace data (AES-256-GCM)
Description:
Relationship data (WhatsApp drafts, promises, date plans) stored in plaintext SQLite and backed up unencrypted to Google Drive. This is a privacy risk.

**Requirements:**
* Use Node.js `crypto.createCipheriv` with AES-256-GCM on the `03_SAHIBA` namespace
* Encryption key derived from env variable `SAHIBA_ENCRYPTION_KEY`
* Wrap `addConversationMessage` and `getRecentConversation` in `core/db.js` for the `03-sahibabot` namespace with encrypt/decrypt
* Add `SENSITIVE: true` flag to the registry config for namespaces that should be excluded from Google Drive backup
* Update `scripts/backup-gdrive.sh` to skip SENSITIVE namespaces
* Add `SAHIBA_ENCRYPTION_KEY` to `sample.env` with generation instructions

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `core/filesystem.js`, `agents/03-sahibabot.js`, `core/registry.js`, `sample.env`
**Dependencies:** Node.js crypto (built-in)

Title: Build core/proactive.js — proactive intelligence scheduler engine
Description:
Currently only Architect (morning/evening briefs) and Watchtower run proactively. The other 11 agents sit idle until asked via Slack. A chief of staff should anticipate needs.

**Requirements:**
* New `core/proactive.js` module with a `ProactiveEngine` class
* Add a `proactiveChecks` array to each agent's registry config
* Each check is an object: `{ name, cron, gatherFn, conditionFn, priority, channel }`
* `gatherFn()` collects data (e.g., step count, spending today, calendar events)
* `conditionFn(data)` returns true if the agent should fire (e.g., steps < 5000 by 3pm)
* Only dispatches to `agent.run()` when the condition fires, with gathered data as context
* Respects budget tiers — don't fire proactive checks if agent is budget-paused
* Publishes `hydra:proactive.fired` bus event with agent name and check name
* Start with 4 checks:
  - **BioBot:** 3 PM step nudge (steps < 5000)
  - **CFOBot:** daily spending velocity (> ₹500/day pace)
  - **SahibaBot:** anniversary/date reminders from calendar (within 3 days)
  - **EdmoBot:** standup reminder (no Jira update by 10 AM)

**Files:** new `core/proactive.js`, `core/registry.js`, `ecosystem.config.cjs`
**Dependencies:** node-cron, core/bus.js, core/bottleneck.js, core/db.js

Title: Implement intelligent model routing per request complexity
Description:
Every request uses the agent's fixed model regardless of complexity. Simple queries cost the same as complex multi-step analysis.

**Requirements:**
* Add `routeModel(userMessage, agentConfig)` function in `core/agent.js`
* Classify complexity using heuristics:
  - **Low:** message < 50 chars, no question marks, simple greetings/status checks → use `mistralai/mistral-small-3.2-24b-instruct`
  - **Medium:** standard queries with single-topic focus → use agent's default model
  - **High:** message > 200 chars OR contains keywords like 'analyze', 'compare', 'plan', 'strategy' OR requires tools → use agent's default or upgrade
* Track per-complexity-tier token spend in SQLite for cost analysis
* Add `modelRouting: true/false` flag in registry per agent (some agents should always use their assigned model)
* Log routing decisions: `log.info('Routed to model X (complexity: low)', { agentName })`
* Dashboard API: `GET /api/routing-stats` — show routing distribution

**Files:** `core/agent.js`, `core/bottleneck.js`, `core/registry.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** None

Title: Build comprehensive test suite — core modules (Phase 1)
Description:
Only 3 smoke tests exist for a system that autonomously sends messages, creates tickets, controls appliances, and manages finances.

**Requirements (Phase 1 — core modules):**
* `tests/core/bottleneck.test.js` — budget enforcement logic, circuit breaker trip/reset, tier-based pausing, monthly reset, cost calculation
* `tests/core/agent.test.js` — tool call loop with mock LLM, retry logic, context window truncation, conversation history injection, model routing
* `tests/core/db.test.js` — all CRUD operations (agent_state, transactions, conversation_history, recording_briefs, action_items), schema init, parameterized queries
* `tests/core/bus.test.js` — publish/subscribe, payload serialization, NR trace header injection, error handling on Redis disconnect
* All tests must mock external services (OpenRouter, Slack, Redis, Jira, GitHub)
* Use vitest `describe`/`it`/`expect` pattern
* Target: >80% code coverage on core/ modules
* Add `npm run test:coverage` script to package.json

**Files:** `tests/core/*.test.js`, `vitest.config.js`, `package.json`
**Dependencies:** vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8

Title: Build cross-agent workflow engine (core/workflow.js)
Description:
The Redis bus exists but only carries fire-and-forget events. No orchestration layer chains agents together for multi-step workflows.

**Requirements:**
* New `core/workflow.js` module with a `WorkflowEngine` class
* Define workflows as JSON configuration:
```json
{
  "name": "meeting-to-tickets",
  "trigger": "hydra:recording.triaged",
  "condition": "payload.meetingType === 'work'",
  "steps": [
    { "agent": "01-edmobot", "action": "create_jira_tickets", "input": "payload.actionItems" },
    { "agent": "00-architect", "action": "run", "input": "'Update priorities based on new meeting action items'" }
  ]
}
```
* Steps execute sequentially; each step can transform/filter payload before passing to next
* Error handling: if a step fails, log error, publish `hydra:workflow.error`, skip to next step
* Implement 3 starter workflows:
  - **meeting-to-tickets:** recording.triaged (work) → EdmoBot creates Jira tickets from action items
  - **health-to-environment:** health.alert (low HRV) → Architect adjusts priorities + Jarvis adjusts room
  - **spending-alert-cascade:** budget.warning → CFOBot detailed breakdown → Architect adjusts day plan
* Dashboard page: `/workflows` showing active workflows, execution history, success/failure counts

**Files:** new `core/workflow.js`, `core/bus.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** core/bus.js, core/agent.js, core/registry.js

Title: Build decision journal system in core/db.js
Description:
The system logs agent runs and transactions but has no concept of 'decisions.' When CFOBot flags an expense or the Auditor changes a prompt, there is no systematic way to review whether those decisions were good ones.

**Requirements:**
* New SQLite table `decisions`:
  - `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT`
  - `agent TEXT NOT NULL` — which agent made/recommended the decision
  - `category TEXT` — finance, health, work, relationship, system
  - `decision_text TEXT NOT NULL` — what was decided
  - `rationale TEXT` — why this decision was made
  - `outcome TEXT` — what happened as a result (filled in later)
  - `score INTEGER` — 1-5 rating (filled in during retrospective)
  - `created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`
  - `reviewed_at TEXT` — when retrospective was done
* Add `db.addDecision(agent, category, text, rationale)` function
* Add `db.getUnreviewedDecisions(days)` — decisions not yet scored
* Add `db.scoreDecision(id, outcome, score)` function
* Auditor's weekly reflection includes a decision review pass over unscored decisions
* Dashboard page: `/decisions` with table view, filters by agent/category, scoring interface

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `agents/11-auditor.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** None

Title: Build relationship CRM — contacts table + SocialBot enhancement
Description:
SahibaBot handles one relationship. SocialBot only drafts replies. Nobody tracks the full social graph across emails, WhatsApp, meetings, and audio.

**Requirements:**
* New SQLite table `contacts`:
  - `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT`
  - `name TEXT NOT NULL`
  - `platforms TEXT` — JSON array of platforms (email, whatsapp, slack, gchat)
  - `last_contact TEXT` — ISO timestamp of last interaction
  - `contact_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
  - `topics TEXT` — JSON array of recent conversation topics
  - `follow_ups TEXT` — JSON array of pending follow-up items
  - `relationship_type TEXT` — professional, personal, family, freelance
  - `created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`
  - `updated_at TEXT`
* Add contact extraction to `scripts/gws-sync.js` — extract sender names from emails
* Add contact extraction to `scripts/audio-triage.js` — feed `participants` into contacts table
* SocialBot enhancement: surface "You haven't talked to X in 30 days", "Y promised a proposal — follow up?"
* Dashboard page: `/contacts` with contact list, last interaction, follow-up items

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `agents/04-socialbot.js`, `scripts/audio-triage.js`, `scripts/gws-sync.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** core/gws.js

Title: Build meeting preparation automation
Description:
Calendar events are synced, audio triage extracts action items, email context exists in memory — but nothing stitches them into pre-meeting prep briefs.

**Requirements:**
* Add a proactive check (requires core/proactive.js) that triggers 30 minutes before each calendar event
* Query workflow: attendee names → contacts CRM → recent emails/messages with them → open action items from past meetings
* Generate structured prep brief:
```
📋 Meeting Prep: Sprint Planning with Team
⏰ In 30 minutes (2:00 PM - 3:00 PM)

👥 Attendees:
- John (last talked: 2 days ago, topic: API redesign)
- Sarah (last talked: 5 days ago, topic: hiring)

📧 Recent threads:
- "Re: Q2 roadmap" (3 emails, last: yesterday)
- "Sprint 41 retro notes" (1 email, last: 3 days ago)

✅ Open action items from last meeting:
- [ ] Review PR #245 (assigned to you)
- [ ] Send budget proposal to Sarah

🎯 Suggested talking points:
- Follow up on API redesign with John
- Discuss hiring timeline with Sarah
```
* Post prep brief to `#00-architect` Slack channel
* After meeting: cross-reference new audio triage results with prep brief for follow-up tracking

**Files:** `core/proactive.js` (new check), `core/gws.js`, `agents/00-architect.js`
**Dependencies:** core/proactive.js (#5), contacts CRM (#10), audio-triage

Title: Build goal tracking system with dashboard visualization
Description:
The Architect prompt mentions 3 macro goals (marriage, debt clearance, health) but there is no structured tracking beyond agent_state.

**Requirements:**
* New SQLite table `goals`:
  - `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT`
  - `category TEXT NOT NULL` — finance, health, relationship, career
  - `name TEXT NOT NULL` — e.g., "Clear ₹12.5L debt"
  - `target_value REAL` — numeric target
  - `current_value REAL` — current progress
  - `unit TEXT` — rupees, kg, days, count
  - `deadline TEXT` — ISO date
  - `milestones TEXT` — JSON array of milestone objects
  - `created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`
  - `updated_at TEXT`
* Seed with 3 goals: debt clearance (₹12.5L by Oct 2027), wedding fund, health (weight target)
* Architect evening summary auto-updates goal progress from CFOBot (debt), BioBot (health)
* Dashboard page: `/goals` with visual progress bars, milestone markers, deadline countdown
* Weekly goal review in auditor reflection: "Debt payoff is X% ahead/behind schedule"

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `agents/00-architect.js`, `agents/11-auditor.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** None

Title: Build time blocking and deep work protection system
Description:
The system interrupts equally whether the user is in a 1:1 meeting or deep coding flow. Notifications should be context-aware.

**Requirements:**
* New `agent_state` key: `system.mode` with values: `normal`, `deep_work`, `meeting`, `sleep`
* Mode transitions triggered by:
  - Calendar events (automatic: meeting start/end)
  - Slack command: `@hydra mode deep_work` / `@hydra mode normal`
  - Jarvis automation: `@hydra jarvis sleep mode`
* In `deep_work` mode:
  - Non-urgent Slack messages queue in SQLite `message_queue` table instead of posting
  - Only `Urgent` priority proactive checks fire
  - BioBot delays non-critical health nudges
* In `meeting` mode:
  - Queue all non-urgent messages
  - Audio triage runs with higher priority
* Queue flush: when mode returns to `normal`, post queued messages as a digest
* Slack gateway checks mode before posting: `if (mode === 'deep_work' && !isUrgent) { queue(message) }`
* Dashboard indicator: show current mode in header bar

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `agents/99-slack-gateway.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** core/proactive.js (for calendar-based triggers)

Title: Build habit formation tracker with streaks
Description:
BioBot's prompt mentions quit tracking for smoking/weed but there is no structured habit storage. Streaks and milestones are powerful motivators.

**Requirements:**
* New SQLite table `habits`:
  - `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT`
  - `name TEXT NOT NULL` — e.g., "No smoking", "10K steps", "Meditation"
  - `category TEXT` — health, productivity, personal
  - `streak_current INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
  - `streak_best INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
  - `last_completed TEXT` — ISO date
  - `total_completions INTEGER DEFAULT 0`
  - `frequency TEXT DEFAULT 'daily'` — daily, weekly
  - `auto_track INTEGER DEFAULT 0` — 1 if can be auto-tracked via Apple Health
  - `created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`
* Auto-mark habits from Apple Health data (steps > 10K, sleep > 7h) via health-sync
* Manual check-in via Slack: `@hydra biobot habit meditation done`
* Morning brief includes habit status: current streaks, milestones approaching
* Streak milestones trigger celebration messages (7 days, 30 days, 100 days)
* Dashboard page: `/habits` with streak calendar heatmap, milestone badges

**Files:** `core/db.js`, `agents/07-biobot.js`, `scripts/health-sync.js`, `agents/00-architect.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** health-sync.js

Title: Implement pattern learning from user behavior
Description:
No agent analyzes recurring patterns in user behavior across screen activity, transactions, and calendar.

**Requirements:**
* New weekly batch job `scripts/pattern-analyzer.js` running Sundays at 8 PM
* Analyzes last 7 days of: screen activity summaries, transaction data, calendar events, habit completions
* Uses LLM (Mistral Small, cheapest) to identify patterns:
  - Temporal: "Works late on Wednesdays", "Orders food after 11 PM on Fridays"
  - Behavioral: "Checks LinkedIn every morning", "Skips gym on meeting-heavy days"
  - Financial: "Spends more on weekends", "UPI payments spike after payday"
* Store patterns in `agent_state` as JSON: `{pattern, days, confidence, first_seen, last_seen}`
* Inject relevant patterns into agent system prompts as "Known Behavioral Patterns" section
* Auditor reviews and prunes patterns during weekly reflection (remove low-confidence or stale)
* CFOBot can pre-warn about predicted spending; BioBot can adjust step goals

**Files:** new `scripts/pattern-analyzer.js`, `core/agent.js`, `agents/11-auditor.js`, `ecosystem.config.cjs`
**Dependencies:** core/memory.js, core/db.js

Title: Integrate Wolf with live NSE market data API
Description:
Wolf's F&O analysis uses Perplexity for market 'data' — all analysis is LLM-inferred, not actual prices. Paper trades are based on hallucinated quotes.

**Requirements:**
* New `core/market-data.js` module with functions:
  - `getOptionChain(symbol)` — returns puts/calls with strike prices, premiums, OI, volume
  - `getLTP(symbol)` — returns last traded price for equity/index
  - `getHistorical(symbol, range)` — returns OHLCV candles
  - `getNiftyExpiry()` — returns next expiry date
* Data source: NSE India unofficial API or Zerodha Kite Connect (free tier) or Upstox API
* Add these as tools to Wolf's tool set in the gateway
* Update paper trade recording in `core/db.js` `paper_trades` table to store actual API prices instead of LLM-inferred values
* Rate limiting: max 5 requests/second to avoid IP blocks
* Cache option chain data for 5 minutes (refresh on explicit request)

**Files:** new `core/market-data.js`, `agents/09-wolf.js`, `agents/99-slack-gateway.js`, `core/db.js`
**Dependencies:** axios (for API calls)
**Env vars:** `NSE_API_URL` or `KITE_API_KEY`, `KITE_API_SECRET`

Title: Build email management automation — intelligent triage
Description:
Email tools exist for EdmoBot and SocialBot but there is no intelligent triage layer. No automatic categorization or priority surfacing.

**Requirements:**
* Add LLM classification step to `scripts/gws-sync.js` during email sync:
  - Categories: `action_required`, `fyi`, `promotional`, `automated`, `spam`
  - Priority: `urgent`, `normal`, `low`
* Store classification in new SQLite table `email_triage`:
  - `id, message_id, profile, sender, subject, category, priority, created_at, actioned_at`
* Daily email digest posted to relevant Slack channel:
```
📧 Email Digest (last 24h):
🔴 Action Required (3): [subject1], [subject2], [subject3]
📋 FYI (12): [grouped by sender]
🗑️ Promotional (5): auto-archived
```
* Auto-draft acknowledgment responses for FYI emails (save as drafts, don't send)
* Surface action-required emails in Architect's morning brief
* Dashboard page: `/email` with triage overview, category breakdown, action buttons

**Files:** `scripts/gws-sync.js`, `core/gws.js`, `core/db.js`, `agents/00-architect.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** core/gws.js, core/memory.js

Title: Add Telegram bot interface via Hermes bridge
Description:
Slack mobile is heavy and notification-noisy. A lightweight Telegram bot gives focused push-notification-driven access to HYDRA on the go.

**Requirements:**
* Leverage existing `core/hermes-bridge.js` Telegram support
* Add a Telegram message handler in `agents/99-slack-gateway.js` that mirrors Slack dispatch:
  - Same `hydra <agent> <message>` syntax
  - Route to same agent.run() pipeline
  - Format responses for Telegram markdown
* Support Telegram inline keyboards for approve/reject flows:
  - SocialBot draft approvals
  - Auditor reflection approvals
  - Proactive check acknowledgments
* Add `/status` command in Telegram showing agent health grid
* Notification routing: urgent alerts go to both Slack and Telegram; normal messages Slack only

**Files:** `core/hermes-bridge.js`, `agents/99-slack-gateway.js`
**Dependencies:** Hermes gateway (external)
**Env vars:** `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`

Title: Implement semantic memory deduplication
Description:
Every `agent.run()` adds a memory entry to LanceDB. Many are near-duplicates that crowd out distinct memories and slow searches.

**Requirements:**
* Before inserting a new memory in `core/memory.js` `addMemory()`:
  - Search for top-1 most similar existing memory
  - If cosine similarity > 0.95, update existing memory's timestamp instead of inserting
  - Log: `log.debug('Skipped duplicate memory (similarity: 0.97)', { text: first50chars })`
* Weekly batch job in `scripts/memory-gc.js` (Sunday 3 AM):
  - Scan all memory tables for pairs with similarity > 0.90
  - Merge by keeping the more recent entry, updating its count
  - Delete the older duplicate
  - Report: `Merged N duplicate memories, freed M entries`
* Track memory table sizes in Watchtower health checks
* Dashboard metric: memory table row counts + dedup rate

**Files:** `core/memory.js`, new `scripts/memory-gc.js`, `ecosystem.config.cjs`
**Dependencies:** core/memory.js (embedding cache should be built first for efficiency)

Title: Add response caching for repeated LLM queries
Description:
Repeated identical questions cost full token price each time. Common queries like 'how much did I spend today' are asked multiple times daily.

**Requirements:**
* Before calling `openai.chat.completions.create()` in `core/agent.js`:
  - Hash the user message + truncated context (first 500 chars) to produce a cache key
  - Check Redis for cached response with key prefix `hydra:cache:`
  - If cache hit and response is < 15 minutes old, return cached response
* Only cache non-tool-call responses (tool calls have side effects and must not be cached)
* TTL: 15 minutes for most agents, 5 minutes for CFOBot (financial data changes fast)
* Log cache hits: `log.info('Cache hit for agent query', { agentName, cacheAge })`
* Add cache stats to dashboard: hit rate, entries count, estimated savings

**Files:** `core/agent.js`, `core/bus.js` (Redis connection)
**Dependencies:** ioredis (already installed)

Title: Build document knowledge base ingestion pipeline
Description:
Important documents (contracts, tax papers, learning materials) are not in the memory system. Agents cannot answer questions about them.

**Requirements:**
* New `scripts/doc-ingest.js` pipeline watching `~/hydra-brain/documents/` folder
* Supported formats: PDF (via pdf-parse), plain text, markdown
* Processing pipeline:
  - Detect file type by extension
  - Extract text content
  - Chunk into 512-token segments with 50-token overlap
  - Embed via `core/memory.js` `getEmbedding()`
  - Write to LanceDB `context_feed` table with `source_type: 'document'`
* Deduplication: track ingested files by path + hash in SQLite `ingested_documents` table
* Skip already-ingested files (idempotent)
* PM2 process: `doc-ingest`, watching mode via chokidar
* Support manual re-ingestion: `node scripts/doc-ingest.js --file path/to/doc.pdf`

**Files:** new `scripts/doc-ingest.js`, `ecosystem.config.cjs`
**Dependencies:** pdf-parse (npm), chokidar, core/memory.js

Title: Build BrandBot content calendar with auto-posting workflow
Description:
BrandBot has a creative temperature (0.75) optimized for content generation but never runs autonomously. No proactive personal branding.

**Requirements:**
* Weekly cron (Friday 6 PM): BrandBot analyzes the week's:
  - GitHub contributions (PRs merged, code reviews)
  - Jira tickets completed
  - Audio triage work-related recordings
  - Any notable achievements from Architect logs
* Generates 2-3 draft LinkedIn/Twitter posts with:
  - Professional tone, technical depth
  - Relevant hashtags
  - 1-2 sentence hook + 3-4 sentence body
* Saves drafts to `agent_state` with status `draft`
* Posts drafts to `#02-brandbot` Slack channel with approve/edit/discard buttons
* On approval: either post directly to LinkedIn (via LinkedIn API) or save final text for manual posting
* Track posting frequency and engagement (manual input) in SQLite `content_posts` table

**Files:** `agents/02-brandbot.js`, `agents/99-slack-gateway.js`, `core/db.js`
**Dependencies:** core/github.js, core/jira.js

Title: Replace file-based agent eviction with Redis bus hot-reload
Description:
The current agent eviction mechanism checks `EVICT_FILE` timestamps in the gateway — it is fragile and races with concurrent requests.

**Requirements:**
* Add new Redis bus event: `hydra:agent.reconfigure`
* Payload: `{ agentName, reason, timestamp }`
* Gateway subscribes to the event and atomically replaces the cached agent instance
* Dashboard `POST /api/agent/{name}/model` and `POST /api/agent/{name}/config` publish the bus event instead of writing an evict file
* Add a version counter to agent instances to detect stale references during concurrent requests
* Remove the EVICT_FILE polling logic from `agents/99-slack-gateway.js` (lines 698-709)

**Files:** `agents/99-slack-gateway.js`, `core/bus.js`, `scripts/dashboard.js`
**Dependencies:** core/bus.js (Redis)

Title: Add backup verification and automated restore testing
Description:
The backup script runs daily at 2 AM but there is no verification that backups are valid or that restores work.

**Requirements:**
* After each backup completes in `scripts/backup-gdrive.sh`:
  - Download the latest backup file from Google Drive
  - Verify file integrity via SHA256 checksum comparison
  - Verify SQLite backup opens correctly: `sqlite3 backup.db 'SELECT count(*) FROM agent_state'`
  - Log verification result and post to Slack
* Monthly automated restore test (1st of month, 4 AM):
  - Download latest backup to temp directory
  - Restore SQLite DB and verify table counts match production
  - Report discrepancies to `#hydra-alerts`
* Watchtower alert if backup has not completed in 24 hours
* Track backup sizes over time (sudden drops indicate data loss)

**Files:** `scripts/backup-gdrive.sh`, `scripts/watchdog.js`
**Dependencies:** Google Drive CLI (rclone), sqlite3

Title: Create proper agents/07-biobot.js with proactive health monitoring
Description:
BioBot currently has a prompt but no dedicated agent file with cron-driven health checks. It only responds when asked.

**Requirements:**
* Create `agents/07-biobot.js` with cron schedules:
  - 6:30 AM: morning readiness check (sleep quality from Apple Health)
  - 3:00 PM: afternoon step check (steps < 5000 → nudge)
  - 9:00 PM: evening health summary (steps, active calories, HRV if available)
* Read Apple Health data from brain directory written by `scripts/health-sync.js`
* Check screen context for stress indicators (keywords: deadline, urgent, behind schedule)
* Craving intervention: if screen shows food delivery apps + time > 10 PM → send healthy alternative suggestion
* Post proactive nudges to `#07-biobot` Slack channel
* Respect system mode: suppress non-urgent nudges in `deep_work` and `meeting` modes
